title: Paper argues coding agents could replace mandatory human code review
friendlyTitle: A research paper questions the future of human code review
summary: A new arXiv paper, The End of Code Review: Coding Agents Supersede Human Inspection, argues that LLM-based coding agents can serve the goals of traditional code review at lower cost and higher throughput. The author contends that requiring humans to review all AI-generated code may become an assurance bottleneck rather than a safeguard.
friendlySummary: A paper argues that coding agents may soon handle many jobs that human code reviewers do today, especially as AI writes more code.
whyItMatters: Whether or not the thesis is too strong, it captures a real workflow pressure: AI can generate changes faster than humans can inspect them. Engineering teams will need new quality gates built around tests, agents, provenance, and targeted human review rather than blanket approvals.
friendlyWhyItMatters: Software teams may need new ways to check code because AI can create changes very quickly. Humans may review the riskiest parts instead of every line.
